name: Yellow Party Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream sugar and butter together in a mixer bowl until light and fluffy, about 8 to 10 minutes at medium speed.
2. Add eggs and vanilla; beat for an additional 2 minutes or until mixture is light and fluffy.
3. Scrape the bowl frequently.
4. Stir together flour, baking powder, and salt. Add to creamed mixture alternately with milk, beating on low speed after each addition.
5. Beat for 1 minute at the end.
6. Pour batter into two greased and lightly floured 9x11-inch round baking pans.
7. Bake in a 350°F oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Cool the cakes on wire racks for 10 minutes.
9. Remove from pans and cool thoroughly.
10. Fill with Lemon Filling and frost with Seven-Minute Frosting.
11. Sprinkle shredded coconut on sides of cake, if desired.
Lemon Filling:
Combine 3/4 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons cornstarch, and a dash of salt in a saucepan. Add 3/4 cup water, 2 beaten egg yolks, and 3 tablespoons lemon juice. Cook over medium heat until thick and bubbly, stirring constantly. Remove from heat; stir in 1 teaspoon grated lemon peel and 1 tablespoon butter or margarine.
Seven-Minute Frosting:
In a double boiler (top not over direct heat), combine 1 1/2 cups sugar, 2 beaten egg whites, 2 teaspoons light corn syrup, and a dash of salt. Add 1/3 cup cold water. Beat for 1/2 minute at low speed to blend.
Place the top of the double boiler over boiling water (the bottom should not touch the water). Beat constantly with an electric mixer until the frosting forms stiff peaks, about 7 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in 1 teaspoon vanilla. Beat for an additional 2 minutes or until spreading consistency is reached. Spread on cake immediately.
